lines.speaker: Ur-zek
lines.text: I think that Ur-tag is trying to move too quickly. First, he opens the city to humans, and now he wants to invite these dwarves into the city. I think we should exercise more caution in dealing with surface-dwellers.

lines.speaker: Ur-taal
lines.text: The dwarves are not surface dwellers. They live in an underground city, like us!

lines.speaker: Ur-zek
lines.text: Why does that mean we can trust them? Are you forgetting the machine that nearly destroyed us was made by dwarves?

lines.speaker: Ur-taal
lines.text: You shouldn't judge all dwarves because of that!

lines.speaker: Ur-zek
lines.text: I know that! And I know not all humans want to kill us, but I still never thought we should open the city.

lines.speaker: Ur-taal
lines.text: You can't deny it's done some good. Think of how happy everyone is trying surface foods in the market!

lines.speaker: Ur-zek
lines.text: I'm not denying that, I just don't think it's worth the risk. Our guards have their work cut out as it is, protecting us from renegade humans, without having to worry about dwarves as well.

lines.speaker: Ur-taal
lines.text: Please don't be offended by my friend's attitude, surface-dweller. I'm sure you understand why we need to be cautious.

sections.title: If Ur-zek is currently giving market permission

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: May I have permission to sell food in the market?
